DefinitionDescription: Measuring both the IgGκ and IgGλ, calculating the IgGκ/IgGλ ratio, and then comparing with normal subjects levels may provide a more sensitive indication of clonality. Usage of the IgGκ/IgGλ ratio may also compensate for any fluctuations in plasma volume and adjust for half-life variations. This term was based on, but not limited in use to, the Hevylite Human IgG Kappa and Lambda assays.
FORMULA: IgG kappa (unit g/L) is divided by IgG lambda (unit: g/L) to calculate the IgG kappa/lambda-ratio.

DefinitionDescription: An elevated kappa/lambda ratio supports the diagnosis of a monoclonal gammopathy and requires further investigation. Borderline elevated kappa/lambda ratios occur with renal impairment, and may require appropriate renal function tests. Elevated levels of both kappa and lambda with normal kappa/lambda ratio - may be due to (1) renal impairment (common); (2) Over-production of polyclonal FLC from inflammatory conditions (common); (3) Bi-clonal gammopathies of different free light chain types (rare).
